report_name: Dutch Nationals Suspected in Odido Data Breach Affecting 6 Million Customers

threat_actors.name: Dutch Threat Actors
threat_actors.description: A group of Dutch-speaking criminals suspected of conducting a vishing and data breach attack against the telecommunications provider Odido by impersonating an IT employee over the phone to gain initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Voice
attack_patterns.description: The attackers gained initial access via a voice phishing (vishing) call, where a Dutch-speaking individual impersonated an IT employee to deceive a customer service agent.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The overall attack strategy involved a social engineering and phishing campaign to deceive an employee into granting access to internal systems.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: After successfully obtaining credentials via vishing, the attackers used the legitimate account to access an internal customer contact system.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The attackers exfiltrated a dataset containing the personal information of six million customers from a compromised internal customer contact system.

identities.name: Odido
identities.description: A major Dutch telecommunications provider that suffered a data breach affecting approximately six million customers after a successful vishing attack.

identities.name: Dutch High Tech Crime Team (THTC)
identities.description: The Dutch law enforcement agency leading the criminal investigation into the Odido data breach.

indicators.name: Potential Bulk Data Query from Customer Database
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Bulk Data Query from Customer Database
id: f9a8b7c6-d5e4-4f3a-2b1c-0a9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects a database query that may indicate a bulk export of customer data, a technique used to exfiltrate information after initial access. This behavior was central to the Odido data breach. This rule is a template and requires customization for the specific database logging environment.
references:
  - https://securityaffairs.com/195235/cyber-crime/dutch-nationals-suspected-in-odido-hack-that-exposed-six-million-customers.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-13
tags:
  - attack.t1048
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: database
detection:
  selection:
    statement|contains|all:
      - 'SELECT'
      - 'FROM'
      - 'customers'
  filter:
    statement|contains: 'LIMIT 1'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects database queries that may indicate a bulk export of customer data, a technique used to exfiltrate information after initial access. This behavior was highlighted as a hunting hint in the Odido data breach report.

relationships.source_name: Potential Bulk Data Query from Customer Database
